title: Value Investing and Behavioral Finance
subtitle: Insights into Intrinsic Shareholder Value and Market Sentiments
author: Prem C. Jain
themes: Markets are not fully efficient — psychological factors drive mispricings
Cognitive biases as systematic sources of investment error
The five disciplines of value investing
Graham-Dodd-Buffett tradition applied to modern markets
Sustainable competitive advantage (economic moats) as value anchors
How market overreactions create opportunities for disciplined investors
Social proof, herd behavior, and sentiment-driven market swings
excerpt: Markets are not fully efficient. Understanding the psychological forces that drive investor behavior — overconfidence, anchoring, social proof — is not a sideshow to value analysis; it IS the next step in understanding when and why intrinsic value diverges from market price.
